Senior Product Operations Analyst

Northstar Health is looking for a Senior Product Operations Analyst to help our
product, design, engineering, customer success, and clinical operations teams
make better decisions as we expand our care coordination platform across new
markets. This role sits at the center of several fast-moving teams, so we are
looking for someone who is comfortable translating ambiguity into clear
operating rhythms, useful reporting, and practical recommendations. The ideal
candidate has worked in a software or health technology environment before,
understands how product teams operate, and enjoys improving the systems behind
the work as much as the work itself.

In this position, you will partner closely with product managers to understand
roadmap priorities, customer feedback, launch timelines, and operational
constraints. You will be expected to synthesize information from multiple
sources, including support tickets, customer interviews, CRM records, analytics
dashboards, sales feedback, implementation notes, and internal planning
documents. Much of the work will involve identifying patterns, surfacing risks
early, and helping teams decide where process changes, tooling improvements, or
clearer communication would have the biggest impact.

A successful person in this role will be highly organized without being rigid.
You should be able to create repeatable processes for roadmap intake, release
readiness, customer feedback triage, and post-launch measurement, while still
adapting those processes when the business needs change. We do not expect
perfection, but we do expect someone who can build structure, document
decisions, and follow through consistently. Strong written communication is
important because many of your recommendations will need to be shared with
stakeholders who do not have time to dig through raw data or long meeting notes.

You will spend a meaningful amount of time working with data. This does not mean
you need to be a data scientist, but you should be comfortable querying or
analyzing product and operational data, spotting inconsistencies, and explaining
what the numbers do and do not prove. Experience with SQL is strongly preferred,
and experience with BI tools such as Looker, Tableau, Mode, or Power BI would be
helpful. You should also be comfortable using spreadsheets for quick analysis,
scenario modeling, and operational tracking. We are especially interested in
candidates who can combine quantitative findings with qualitative customer
feedback rather than relying on one source of truth.

Because our product supports clinical workflows, attention to detail matters.
You will help ensure launches are coordinated across product, engineering,
customer-facing teams, and operational stakeholders. That may include preparing
launch checklists, tracking dependencies, confirming enablement materials are
complete, monitoring early adoption, and coordinating follow-up when customers
experience confusion or friction. Prior experience in healthcare, care delivery,
insurance, compliance-heavy environments, or other regulated industries would be
valuable, though it is not strictly required if you have strong product
operations experience elsewhere.

The role requires strong cross-functional collaboration. You should be
comfortable facilitating meetings, asking clarifying questions, resolving
conflicting inputs, and helping teams leave discussions with clear owners and
next steps. You will not manage engineers or designers directly, but you will
influence how work moves through the organization. Candidates who have
experience supporting agile product teams, working with Jira or Linear,
maintaining product documentation in tools like Confluence or Notion, and
coordinating release planning across multiple teams will likely be successful
here.

We are looking for someone with at least five years of experience in product
operations, business operations, product analytics, program management,
consulting, or a similar role supporting software teams. Experience in a B2B
SaaS company is preferred, especially if you have worked with enterprise
customers or complex implementation cycles. You should be comfortable operating
independently, prioritizing your own work, and knowing when to escalate
decisions. This is a senior individual contributor role, so we expect you to
bring judgment, not just task execution.

Day to day, your work might include building a dashboard to track feature
adoption after a major release, reviewing customer feedback to identify
recurring workflow pain points, preparing a concise summary of launch risks for
leadership, improving the product intake process, or helping a product manager
understand whether a requested feature represents a one-off customer need or a
broader market pattern. You may also help maintain operating cadences such as
quarterly planning, roadmap reviews, beta program tracking, customer advisory
board follow-ups, and post-launch retrospectives.

The strongest candidates will be curious, pragmatic, and clear communicators.
You should be able to move from detailed analysis to executive summary without
losing the thread. You should enjoy making messy information usable. You should
also be comfortable saying when the available data is incomplete, when a
recommendation is based on judgment, and when a team needs to make a decision
despite uncertainty.

This is a remote-friendly role based in the United States, though candidates
must be able to work core hours aligned with Eastern or Central time zones.
Occasional travel, likely two to four times per year, may be required for team
planning sessions, customer meetings, or company gatherings. Compensation will
depend on experience and location, and the role includes health benefits, paid
time off, a home office stipend, and participation in the company's annual bonus
program.

To apply, please send a resume and a short note describing a product or
operational process you improved, what problem you were solving, and how you
knew the change worked.
